findings.evidence: `rg` shows the adjacent quotient API uses `liftQuotient` for exactly this construction: `HopfIdealQuotient.lean:95 noncomputable abbrev liftQuotient ...`, `:108 lemma liftQuotient_mk`, `:116 lemma mkQuotient_comp_liftQuotient`; the new names are at `HopfIdealPoints.lean:92`, `:104`, and `:117`.
findings.fix: Rename these along the adjacent API, e.g. `liftQuotientPoint`, `liftQuotientPoint_mk`, and `quotientPointsHom_liftQuotientPoint` or similar lift/factor-through-quotient names.
findings.issue: `quotientPointOfKills` names the hypothesis that the ambient point kills `I`, not the conclusion that it is the lift/factorization through the quotient; the same nonstandard stem appears in `quotientPointOfKills_apply_mk` and `quotientPointsHom_quotientPointOfKills`.

summary: One public constructor and its companion lemmas use a proof-condition name instead of the established quotient-lift terminology.
